---
title: "Protocol 2: Self-Prediction"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
params:
  samples_path: "outputs/viz/default/samples.parquet"
  agg_path: "outputs/viz/default/aggregated.parquet"
---

```{python}
#| tags: [parameters]
samples_path = "outputs/viz/default/samples.parquet"
agg_path = "outputs/viz/default/aggregated.parquet"
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path

project_root = Path(".").resolve().parent
if str(project_root) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(project_root))

def _resolve_path(p: str) -> str:
    pp = Path(p)
    if pp.is_absolute() and pp.exists():
        return str(pp)
    candidate = project_root / p
    if candidate.exists():
        return str(candidate)
    candidate = Path(".").resolve() / p
    if candidate.exists():
        return str(candidate)
    return p

agg_path_resolved = _resolve_path(agg_path)
samples_path_resolved = _resolve_path(samples_path)

import pandas as pd
from inspect_viz import Data
from inspect_viz.input import select
from inspect_viz.view import scores_by_factor
from inspect_viz.mark import area_y, line
from inspect_viz.plot import legend, plot
from inspect_viz.transform import ci_bounds
from inspect_viz.table import table, column as tcol
from inspect_viz.layout import vconcat, hconcat

from scripts.viz_helpers import trajectory_plot

_agg_df = pd.read_parquet(agg_path_resolved)
_has_prediction = "instruction_following_binary_mean" in _agg_df.columns

METRICS = [
    ("instruction_following_binary_mean", "instruction_following_binary_stderr", "Instruction Following (Actual)"),
    ("prediction_accuracy_binary_mean", "prediction_accuracy_binary_stderr", "Prediction Accuracy"),
    ("prediction_instruction_binary_mean", "prediction_instruction_binary_stderr", "Prediction Instruction (Says Will Follow)"),
]
# Filter to metrics actually present in data
METRICS = [(s, se, t) for s, se, t in METRICS if s in _agg_df.columns]
```

## Overview

Protocol 2 tests whether models can accurately predict their own behavior before generating a response. Three metrics:

- **Instruction Following**: Did the actual output follow the instruction (T)?
- **Prediction Accuracy**: Did the prediction match the actual output?
- **Prediction Instruction**: Did the prediction say it would follow the instruction (T)?

```{python}
#| echo: false
if not _has_prediction:
    from IPython.display import Markdown
    display(Markdown("*No prediction data available. Run a prediction protocol evaluation first.*"))
```

## Trajectories: Score vs N {#sec-trajectory}

Each line represents a separate model. Three metrics shown vertically.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if _has_prediction:
    traj_data = Data.from_file(agg_path_resolved)

    hconcat(
        select(traj_data, label="Condition Pair", column="condition_pair"),
        select(traj_data, label="Hint", column="hint"),
    )
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if _has_prediction:
    vconcat(
        *[
            trajectory_plot(
                traj_data,
                score_col=score_col,
                stderr_col=stderr_col,
                title=title,
                stroke_col="model",
                height=300,
            )
            for score_col, stderr_col, title in METRICS
        ]
    )
```

## Factor Comparison {#sec-factor}

Pick a condition pair and N to compare aligned vs. misaligned per model for each metric.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if _has_prediction:
    factor_data = Data.from_file(agg_path_resolved)

    hconcat(
        select(factor_data, label="Condition Pair", column="condition_pair", value="auto"),
        select(factor_data, label="Hint", column="hint"),
        select(factor_data, label="N", column="n_turns", value="auto"),
    )
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if _has_prediction:
    vconcat(
        *[
            scores_by_factor(
                factor_data,
                factor="instruction_aligned",
                factor_labels=("Misaligned", "Aligned"),
                model="model",
                score_value=score_col,
                score_stderr=stderr_col,
                score_label=title,
                title=title,
                width=900,
            )
            for score_col, stderr_col, title in METRICS
        ]
    )
```

## Calibration: Predicted vs Actual {#sec-calibration}

Solid lines = actual instruction-following rate. Dashed lines = predicted rate. Per model, filtered by condition pair.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if _has_prediction and "prediction_instruction_binary_mean" in _agg_df.columns:
    cal_data = Data.from_file(agg_path_resolved)

    select(cal_data, label="Condition Pair", column="condition_pair", value="auto")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if _has_prediction and "prediction_instruction_binary_mean" in _agg_df.columns:
    ci_lo_act, ci_hi_act = ci_bounds(
        "instruction_following_binary_mean", level=0.95,
        stderr="instruction_following_binary_stderr",
    )

    plot(
        line(
            cal_data,
            x="n_turns",
            y="instruction_following_binary_mean",
            stroke="model",
            tip=True,
            channels={
                "N": "n_turns",
                "Actual IF": "instruction_following_binary_mean",
                "Model": "model",
            },
        ),
        line(
            cal_data,
            x="n_turns",
            y="prediction_instruction_binary_mean",
            stroke="model",
            stroke_dash="model",
            tip=True,
            channels={
                "N": "n_turns",
                "Predicted IF": "prediction_instruction_binary_mean",
                "Model": "model",
            },
        ),
        area_y(
            cal_data,
            x="n_turns",
            y="instruction_following_binary_mean",
            y1=ci_lo_act,
            y2=ci_hi_act,
            fill="model",
            fill_opacity=0.08,
        ),
        x_label="N (hardcoded turns)",
        y_label="Rate",
        title="Calibration: Solid = Actual, Dashed = Predicted",
        legend=legend("color", frame_anchor="top-right", inset=10),
        width=900,
        height=400,
        y_domain=[0, 1.05],
        color_domain="fixed",
    )
```

## Data Table {#sec-table}

```{python}
#| echo: false
if _has_prediction:
    table_data = Data.from_file(agg_path_resolved)

    cols = [
        tcol("model", label="Model"),
        tcol("condition_pair", label="Pair"),
        tcol("condition", label="Condition"),
        tcol("n_turns", label="N", width=60),
        tcol("instruction_aligned", label="Aligned", width=80),
        tcol("hint", label="Hint", width=60),
    ]
    for score_col, stderr_col, title in METRICS:
        short = title.split("(")[0].strip() if "(" in title else title
        cols.append(tcol(score_col, label=short, width=90))

    table(
        table_data,
        columns=cols,
        sorting=True,
        height="auto",
    )
```
